title: Commercial Finance Business Partner - Enabling
description:
**COMMERCIAL FINANCE BUSINESS PARTNER \- ENANBLING**  


**What You’ll Be Doing**  



We are looking for a person who wants to undertake a professional path starting from the role of **Commercial Finance Business Partner \- Enabling**  



The role is part of the Financial Business Partnering team, reporting to the Head of FBP Commercial. He/she acts as go\-to contact for Procurement teams, Directors of different department and their teams, supporting them in definition, controlling and management of budgets  



**Your Activities Will Include**


* Be responsible for the monthly closing process, providing robust variance analysis, clear financial insights, and identifying cost optimization opportunities.
* Act as a Finance Business Partner to budget owners and the leadership team, supporting decision‑making through strong financial narratives and executive‑level communication.
* Develop business cases for commercial investments, perform post‑project evaluations and challenge stakeholders to maximise spend effectiveness and efficiency.
* Provide actionable financial insights to improve resource allocation and continuously enhance commercial performance.
* Manage and track commercial CAPEX, ensuring disciplined governance, accurate reporting and compliance with internal processes.
* Lead and develop one direct report, fostering a performance‑driven, improvement‑oriented mindset.
* Drive continuous improvement of controlling processes, reporting and ways of working.

  



 **Who We’re Looking For**


* Master’s degree: preferably in Business Administration or Industrial Engineering
* At least 4 years of experience in role of Financial Planning \& Accounting, within international FMCG industry preferably
* Profincency in English and Italian \- written and spoken.
* Advanced MS Office Skills
* Capacity to work in a cross functional team and manage complex situations.
* Strong determination and results driven

  



**In Return We Offer**


* Permanent contract
* Flexible way of working (option to work up to 11 days remotely, without set days)
* Cool new offices
* The opportunity to contribute to impactful projects
* A dynamic work experience in an international context and the chance to learn

  



**Where?**

Our Roma Headquarter!

  



**Environment That You Will find**



We are committed to nurturing a workplace where we celebrate and respect differences, and support everyone to be true to who they are! At Asahi Europe and International we believe you can SHINE AS YOU ARE \- no matter what your age, gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disabilities, religion, or beliefs. We want to support you to be courageous in your individuality and to know that whoever you are and whatever your circumstances, you can belong with us without having to conform.

  


**What next?**


* Our Talent Acquisition Team will assess your application and if you have what we’re looking for, you'll be contacted for an initial phone call.
* If we like what we hear, you’ll be invited to an interview with a member of the Talent Acquisition team and your future Manager!

title: Commis Waiter/Waitress
description:
Come Commis Waiter/Waitress, supporterai il team di servizio e del pass, assicurandoti che ogni piatto venga consegnato con professionalità e che ogni ospite si senta accolto e valorizzato. Sarai una figura chiave nel garantire l’eccezionale esperienza gastronomica di Zuma! Questo lavoro è a tempo pieno e devi avere il diritto di lavorare in Italia a tempo pieno.


Zuma è una catena internazionale di ristoranti di cucina giapponese contemporanea — un brand ispirato allo stile informale e conviviale dell’izakaya. Questo ruolo è basato a Zuma Roma, situato nell’iconico Palazzo Fendi, con un ristorante mozzafiato e una splendida terrazza panoramica con vista sulla “città eterna”: un’esperienza imperdibile a Roma. Zuma Roma fa orgogliosamente parte dell’Azumi Collection.


Mansioni principali


Le tue principali responsabilità includeranno:

* Offrire un servizio cordiale ed efficiente, facendo sentire ogni ospite benvenuto e apprezzato
* Consegnare i piatti ai tavoli con precisione e mantenere pulita e organizzata l’area del pass
* Aiutare nella preparazione del ristorante prima del servizio e seguire le routine di pulizia per mantenere standard elevati
* Partecipare alla formazione per migliorare la conoscenza del menù e sviluppare competenze professionali
* Condividere i feedback degli ospiti e segnalare eventuali problematiche ai manager per garantire un funzionamento fluido
* Supportare nelle operazioni di apertura e chiusura, seguendo le procedure aziendali per garantire un servizio impeccabile


Cosa cerchiamo


Il nostro candidato ideale incarna i nostri valori e possiede:

* Un autentico amore per l’esperienza culinaria e una passione per la cucina giapponese
* Esperienza comprovata come commis di sala in un ristorante di lusso ad alto volume
* Spirito di squadra e attitudine al lavoro in sinergia con un grande team
* Cordialità naturale, orientamento al cliente e capacità di creare esperienze memorabili per gli ospiti


Benefici


Offriamo straordinarie opportunità di carriera, esperienze internazionali e la possibilità di contribuire a un brand di fama mondiale, dove ogni dettaglio – dagli ingredienti più pregiati all’atmosfera senza pari – è curato alla perfezione.


Far parte del nostro team significa poter usufruire di:

* Formazione di livello mondiale, pensata per ispirare ed educare
* Opportunità globali, vivi l’ospitalità in tutto il mondo con i nostri cinque incredibili brand
* Staff meal, condivisi quotidianamente
* Premi e riconoscimenti, valorizziamo il nostro team e celebriamo i suoi contributi con riconoscimenti significativi
* Sconti per lo staff, approfitta di sconti esclusivi in tutto il gruppo Azumi, in tutto il mondo!


Il nostro impegno per l’inclusività


Siamo un datore di lavoro che promuove le pari opportunità e accogliamo con favore tutte le candidature. Se hai bisogno di assistenza per rendere il processo di selezione più accessibile, informa il nostro team di Talent Acquisition.

  


INDFOH

title: Junior Recruiter | Space Sector
description:
**WHO WE ARE**
==============


Etinars is a **values\-focused** company with **multi\-year experience**, specialised in the recruitment of professionals for **niche markets**, managing the **full life\-cycle** of specialist and **executive\-level hires**.  

At Etinars, we genuinely care about who you are and what you need.  

We place great emphasis on fostering **robust**, **enduring connections** built upon **trust** and **transparency**.  

Our approach ensures a **swift journey**, leading you hand in hand towards signing your next **career step**.


**WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR**
===========================


**Junior Recruiter \| Space Sector**
------------------------------------


Join our client’s **international team**, supporting **talent acquisition activities** within programmes linked to **European institutions** and the **space** and **aerospace sectors**.


Our client is a trusted leader in **Space Services**, with a proud history spanning over **55 years** and a track record of supporting more than **50 space missions** for world\-renowned agencies such as **ESA** and **NASA**. Their expertise covers the entire **space lifecycle** \- from **satellite testing** and **launch services** to **spacecraft operations**, **space surveillance**, and **data management** \- making them a key partner in advancing national strategies and driving innovation in the **New Space era**.


We are looking for a **motivated**, **detail\-oriented** **Junior Recruiter** with **1\-2 years of experience** (**internships included**) and a **strong interest in the space or aerospace sector**, to join the **Talent Acquisition team** and support the hiring needs of rapidly growing **space** and **aerospace programmes**.


For this role, candidates living far from the work location must be **willing to relocate** near our client’s office in **Italy (Rome), Czech Republic (Prague), Spain (Madrid), or the Netherlands (Noordwijk)**, unless they are able to **commute regularly.**


A **B2 level of English is mandatory**, while **C1 is strongly preferred**. Additional languages are a plus.


**YOUR TASKS**
==============


In this role, you will contribute to the **full recruitment cycle** for both **technical** and **non\-technical positions**, supporting the attraction of talent across areas such as **engineering**, **mission operations**, **satellite systems**, **software development**, and other key domains of the **space industry**.


Your responsibilities will include:


### **Talent Acquisition \& Sourcing**


* Supporting **end\-to\-end recruitment activities** within the **space domain**.
* Actively sourcing candidates through **LinkedIn**, **job boards**, **university networks**, **events**, and **industry\-specific platforms**.
* Screening CVs and conducting initial **phone/video interviews** to assess **technical** and **cultural fit**.
* Maintaining and growing a **pipeline of qualified candidates** for recurring roles.
* Collaborating with **Senior Recruiters** and **Hiring Managers** to understand role requirements and define **sourcing strategies**.

### **Candidate Management**


* Ensuring a smooth and positive **candidate experience** throughout the hiring process.
* Coordinating **interviews**, **assessments**, and **follow\-up communications**.
* Providing timely **feedback** to candidates and hiring teams.
* Supporting the **negotiation** and preparation of **job offers**, when required.

### **Employer Branding \& Events**


* Contributing to **employer branding initiatives** targeted at the **space/aerospace talent community**.
* Participating in **job fairs**, **university career days**, and **industry events**.

### **Data \& Process Management**


* Maintaining accurate and up\-to\-date records in the **Applicant Tracking System (ATS)**.
* Tracking **recruitment metrics** such as **time\-to\-hire**, **pipeline data**, and **sourcing effectiveness**.
* Supporting the **continuous improvement** of recruitment processes and tools.
* Ensuring compliance with **internal policies** and **employment regulations**.

**YOUR SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE**
==============================


* **Bachelor’s degree** in **Human Resources**, **Psychology**, **Business Administration**, or a related field.
* **1\-2 years of experience** in **recruitment**, **talent acquisition**, or **HR** (**internships included**).
* Strong interest in the **space** or **aerospace sector**.
* Excellent **communication** and **interpersonal skills**.
* Ability to manage **multiple searches simultaneously** in a **fast\-paced environment**.
* Good understanding of **sourcing tools** such as **LinkedIn Recruiter**, **job boards**, and **Boolean search**.
* Proficiency in **English**; additional languages are a plus.

### **Preferred Qualifications**


* Previous experience recruiting **technical profiles** (**engineering**, **IT**, **R\&D**).
* Familiarity with **ATS platforms** and **HR tools**.
* Knowledge of basic concepts related to **space missions**, **satellite technologies**, **aerospace engineering** or **STEM fields**.
* Strong **problem\-solving** and **organisational skills**.

**EXTRA INFORMATION**
=====================


**Type of contract:** **Permanent**  

**Work mode:** **Hybrid** **\- 2 working days per week** in the office and the rest from home  

**Location:** Our client’s office either in **Italy (Rome)**, **Czech Republic (Prague)**, **Spain (Madrid)**, or **Netherlands (Noordwijk)**  

**Starting date: ASAP**  

**Work environment:** **Dynamic**, **innovative**, and **international**, at the forefront of the **space industry**  

**Professional growth:** Opportunities for **professional development** and **continuous training**  

**Exposure:** Direct exposure to **cutting\-edge technical roles** and **international talent**  

**Team:** Collaborative **HR team** and supportive company culture


**Eligibility:**


* **EU Citizens**, or
* **Non\-EU Citizens** already based in the EU with a valid **work permit**


Once we receive your CV, we will take time to evaluate it carefully. Should there be a match for this or other positions at Etinars, we will be in touch.


At Etinars, we are committed to supporting a **Diverse and Inclusive Community**. If you meet the minimum criteria, you will be given the opportunity to demonstrate your abilities during the interview process.

title: Field Sales Partner Enterprise - Lazio (x/f/m)
description:
Doctolib è alla ricerca di un/una **Field Sales Partner con target Enterprise** per unirsi al team e sviluppare le attività commerciali in **Lazio.**



Come **Field Sales Partner** **Enterprise**, farai parte di un team di professionisti impegnati sul campo a semplificare, attraverso le nostre soluzioni, la vita del personale sanitario e a migliorare l'accesso alla sanità di tutti noi!



In questo ruolo, sarai il primo punto di contatto tra **Doctolib** e le **strutture sanitarie** (e.g., poliambulatori, centri medici, imprenditori sanitari) e seguirai tutto il ciclo di vendita dei servizi offerti da Doctolib.



In particolare ti occuperai di:


**Prospecting:**


* Attivare nuove opportunità commerciali contattando poliambulatori e centri medici tramite telefono, email e canali digitali
* Qualificare i prospect identificando decision maker, bisogni, priorità e potenziale, trasformando i contatti in appuntamenti di qualità
* Pianificare follow\-up efficaci per far avanzare le opportunità nel funnel con continuità


**Consulenza e demo prodotto:**


* Guidare incontri per comprendere l'organizzazione della struttura e proporre la soluzione più adatta
* Presentare Doctolib con demo personalizzate, valorizzando benefici concreti per pazienti e staff e gestendo domande/obiezioni


**Negoziazione e chiusura:**


* Costruire e presentare proposte commerciali su misura, guidando la trattativa fino alla firma
* Allineare gli stakeholder interni della struttura e coordinare un handover completo al team di onboarding per un avvio fluido


**Pipeline, metriche e performance:**


* Gestire pipeline e attività su CRM con attenzione alla qualità dei dati e ai next step
* Monitorare KPI e risultati (attività, conversion rate, win rate, durata ciclo di vendita)


Durante tutti gli step di vendita avrai modo di raccogliere feedback sia dai tuoi colleghi/e che dai clienti. Sarà importante per condividere best practices e suggerimenti per migliorare le attività del team Sales e contribuire al successo di Doctolib in Italia.

  


**Cosa ci aspettiamo da te**
----------------------------



Prima di continuare a leggere, se il profilo descritto non è perfettamente aderente al tuo attuale profilo professionale ma ritieni che questa descrizione corrisponda alle tue capacità e alle tue esigenze, ti invitiamo a proseguire!



Questo ruolo potrebbe fare per te se:


* Hai circa 2\-5 anni di esperienza in sales/new business con attività sul field in aziende saas/tech o di prodotti complessi
* Hai un forte orientamento ai risultati, comprendi i KPI e ti impegni a raggiungere e superare gli obiettivi, anche grazie a un bonus competitivo senza cap
* Hai eccellenti doti di comunicazione interpersonale
* Ti distinguono proattività e spiccate doti organizzative
* Energia, determinazione e ambizione sono valori chiave per te
* Ti piace lavorare in team
* Sei in possesso di patente di guida e disponibile a spostamenti nella zona assegnata


Ora, sarebbe fantastico se:


* Hai esperienza o forte interesse per il mondo della sanità
* Hai un buon livello di inglese

  


**Cosa offriamo**
-----------------


* Auto aziendale ad uso promiscuo
* Assicurazione sanitaria integrativa, con la possibilità di includere i familiari conviventi
* Food offer: buoni pasto elettronici (8,00€/giorno lavorato)
* Wellbeing e sport: offriamo servizi per la salute mentale in collaborazione con il nostro partner moka.care e partnership sportiva con Wellhub
* Un programma di supporto alla genitorialità che include diverse iniziative, tra cui il congedo parentale retribuito al 100%
* Per chi assiste familiari non autosufficienti e per i lavoratori con disabilità, è previsto un pacchetto che include un adattamento della policy di lavoro da remoto, giorni aggiuntivi di permesso per motivi sanitari e sostegno psicologico
* Partnership con Ciao Elsa per accesso ai fondi pensione
* Vaccinazione antinfluenzale offerta ogni anno in inverno e diverse iniziative di prevenzione durante l'anno
* Supporto alla mobilità internazionale e accesso ai nostri tool e programmi di upskilling
* Contributo forfettario mensile per internet e elettricità
* Convenzioni aziendali con diversi partner su tutta Italia: Corporate Benefits, Ennevolte, sconti dedicati e promozioni

  


**Il processo di selezione**
----------------------------


* Colloquio con il team di Talent Acquisition
* Colloquio con l'hiring manager
* Colloquio finale
* Offerta!

  


**Descrizione della posizione**
-------------------------------


* Contratto a tempo indeterminato full time
* Area di riferimento: Lazio
* Retribuzione: fissa \+ bonus legati a obiettivi (uncapped).

  


*In Doctolib, ci impegniamo a migliorare l'accesso all'assistenza sanitaria per tutte le persone. Questo si riflette nel nostro processo di selezione. Valutiamo i profili esclusivamente in base alle loro qualifiche e alla loro motivazione, senza nessuna forma di discriminazione.*  

  

*Più si ascolteranno idee diverse, più il nostro prodotto migliorerà veramente l'assistenza sanitaria di ogni utente. Quindi ti diamo il benvenuto e ti invitiamo a candidarti per Doctolib, indipendentemente dal tuo genere, religione, età, orientamento sessuale, etnia o disabilità. Unisciti a noi per costruire un mondo più in salute!*  

  

*Per garantire pari opportunità, ti invitiamo a escludere informazioni personali (ad esempio foto, età) dalle tue candidature. Se hai bisogno di adattamenti particolari per quanto riguarda il processo di selezione, ti preghiamo di informarci quanto prima.*  

  

*Tutte le informazioni fornite vengono trattate da Doctolib per la gestione delle candidature. Per i dettagli sul trattamento dei dati, clicca**qui**. Ti preghiamo di contattare hr.dataprivacy(at)doctolib.com**per domande o per esercitare i tuoi diritti.*

title: Head of People & Organization
description:
Roma / hybrid \| Full\-time \| € 60\.000 – € 90\.000

#### **Who We Are**


We are a growing boutique consulting firm of around 50 people, partnering with companies and public institutions on their digital transformation journeys. We work on complex, end\-to\-end initiatives spanning strategy, service design, delivery, and operational management, bringing together expertise in software development, cloud and on\-prem infrastructure, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and blockchain/DLT.


We operate in a highly knowledge\-intensive environment, where the quality of people, leadership, and organizational systems is a critical factor in growing in a healthy, sustainable, and profitable way.


That is why we are looking for a senior People leader to take ownership of the People function with a hands\-on, managerial, execution\-focused approach. Someone who can support the company’s evolution and create tangible business impact.

#### **The Role**


The person joining us in this role will be responsible for designing, structuring, and leading the company’s People framework, supporting organizational growth and the business across the full employee lifecycle: from attraction and onboarding to development, performance, and offboarding.


We are a company built by people, and our growth has always been driven by the quality of our people and our processes.


We are looking for someone who can take care of both, helping us not only preserve our culture and values, but also evolve and strengthen them over time. This is not a purely operational role, nor a purely strategic one. We are looking for someone who can build processes, drive adoption with managers, measure what matters, and continuously improve the system.

#### **What You Will Do**


Reporting directly to the CEO, you will take full ownership of the People function, building scalable frameworks to support our growth from 50\+ employees onwards.

* **Talent Acquisition \& Onboarding:** Drive the full recruitment funnel for strategic and specialized roles. You will manage both active and passive sourcing, improve assessment quality, and track key recruitment KPIs to ensure a seamless candidate\-to\-employee transition.
* **Performance \& Compensation:** Design and manage the Performance Management system and MBO framework. You will establish a clear Job Architecture with defined levels and growth criteria, ensuring consistency and transparency across all teams.
* **Learning \& Development:** Identify skill gaps and build an L\&D framework (certifications, training, conferences) that aligns individual growth with business priorities and digital transformation trends.
* **Employee Experience \& Culture:** Oversee the entire employee lifecycle, from onboarding to offboarding. You will lead Employer Branding and Internal Brand initiatives, fostering a high\-trust environment through proactive engagement and team\-building activities.
* **People Operations \& Compliance:** Supervise administrative processes, payroll coordination, and external partners. You will ensure operational reliability and legal compliance across all HR activities.
* **Policy Design \& Leadership Support:** Formalize and maintain scalable policies (Hybrid Work, Benefits, Referral, etc.). You will act as a strategic partner to managers, coaching them to adopt these frameworks and improve overall managerial quality.

#### **Strategic Impact \& Success Roadmap**


Success in this role means transitioning the People function from an informal growth phase to a structured, professional engine. Your impact will be measured by the delivery of these core pillars:

* **Data\-Driven Talent Engine**: You will transform recruitment into a predictable, measurable process by establishing and monitoring clear KPIs (such as Time\-to\-Hire and Quality\-of\-Hire).
* **Organizational Architecture:** You will deliver a formalized Level Matrix and Career Path framework that provides clear growth trajectories and is actively adopted by managers to guide team development.
* **Performance \& Accountability:** You will implement a credible Performance Management and MBO system, including the governance of the 20% variable compensation component, ensuring individual goals are strictly aligned with business priorities.
* **Operational Scalability**: You will establish a comprehensive framework of People policies (including L\&D, Hybrid Work, and Benefits) that ensures consistency, compliance, and a high\-performance culture across the organization.

#### **Why Join Us**

* **Real impact:** you will help shape company decisions and improve internal processes, making your work genuinely meaningful
* **Close collaboration with key business leaders:** you will work closely with the company’s strategic functions, with room to learn, contribute ideas, and take part in important decisions
* **Flexible working model:** you will work in a hybrid, flexible environment that values work\-life balance
* **Inclusive environment:** at moveax, we value diversity and foster a workplace where everyone can share ideas and contribute to collective success

#### **What Else You Will Find**


* Flexible working hours
* An amazing team
* If there’s a book you need, we’ll buy it for you
* PlayStation
* Free beer on Fridays
* + Coffee always available
* ✈️ Annual team\-building trips
* Budget for courses, certifications, and industry conferences

#### **Interview Process**


Our selection process is designed to ensure a deep alignment between your professional journey and our scaling challenges. It consists of four distinct stages


Step 1: focus on your background and the scope of your previous People ownership. We will verify the match with core requirements (8\+ years experience, scale\-up environments) and discuss your motivation for joining a boutique consulting firm like Moveax.


Step 2: a strategic conversation with the CEO to evaluate your experience in transitioning organizations from informal to structured. We will explore your "role literacy" regarding technical profiles (Engineering, Cloud, Design) and your vision for the People function in a knowledge\-intensive business.


Step 3: a live business case to evaluate your priority\-setting and decision\-making when building systems from scratch.


Step 4: evaluation of your ability to influence the leadership team and coach managers. We will discuss complex organizational scenarios, conflict resolution, and your overall fit within the senior management team.

#### **Equality \& Inclusion \- Talent Acquisition Policy**


moveax is an equal opportunity employer in accordance with laws 903/77 and 125/91 (regarding gender) and legislative decrees 215/03 and 216/03 (regarding age and nationality). We encourage applications from everyone, regardless of gender, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, age, disability, or any other characteristic.


We firmly believe in creating an inclusive and diverse work environment where every talent has the opportunity to grow and contribute to our success.

 **Experience**

* 8\+ years of experience in People/HR roles, with a proven track record in professional services, technology, or high\-growth digital consulting environments.
* Active participation in scaling a People function (ideally from \~20 to 100\+ employees). You must demonstrate experience in transitioning from "founder\-led" HR to structured, professionalized People operations.
* Deep understanding of the consulting business model. You know how to align HR initiatives with project\-based workflows and knowledge\-intensive productivity.
* Solid familiarity with the professional roles of the digital economy. You speak the language of Application Engineering, Infrastructure/Cloud, and Digital Design (UX/UI, Service Design) and understand their specific career drivers.
* Experience in designing and implementing at least two of the following from scratch: a Performance/MBO framework, a Level/Competency Matrix, or a structured Talent Acquisition strategy.

**️ Hard Skills**

* Proven ability to design and implement Level/Competency Matrices and career paths tailored to professional services.
* Fluency in English and Italian
* Technical expertise in designing MBO systems and managing variable compensation frameworks.
* Deep mastery of data\-driven talent acquisition, including funnel optimization and key recruitment KPIs.
* Solid understanding of Italian labor regulations and oversight of payroll, contracts, and administrative compliance.
* Fluent understanding of the roles, seniority levels, and career drivers within Application Engineering, Cloud Infrastructure, and Digital Design.
* Background in headhunting or executive search (nice to have)

**Soft Skills**

* A "builder" mindset; the ability to shift seamlessly from high\-level strategy to hands\-on operational delivery.
* Strong presence as a strategic partner to the CEO and an authoritative coach to the management team.
* Systemic thinking that ensures every process or policy designed for 50 people remains robust and effective at 150\.
* Absolute discretion in handling sensitive information and a commitment to building a transparent, high\-performance culture.

title: Assistant Restaurant Manager
description:
As an **Assistant Restaurant Manager** at Zuma, you’ll oversee daily operations, inspire the team, and ensure every guest enjoys an outstanding dining experience. Step into a leadership role at Zuma, driving exceptional service and operational excellence!


**Zuma** is a contemporary Japanese restaurant collection, inspired by the dynamic Izakaya dining style. This role will be based in Zuma Rome. Located in the iconic Palazzo Fendi, with a stunning restaurant and beautiful roof terrace overlooking the “eternal city”, this restaurant is an essential experience in Rome. Zuma Rome is proud to be part of the Azumi Collection.

**Main Duties**  

Your key responsibilities will include:




* Delegate and oversee daily tasks, ensuring food and service standards are consistently met
* Handle customer feedback promptly, escalating issues to senior management when needed
* Support initiatives to optimize operations and enhance the guest experience
* Monitor and control costs while maintaining high standards and guest satisfaction
* Ensure strict compliance with health, safety, and hygiene regulations
* Lead daily briefings, conduct training sessions, and provide regular feedback to develop and motivate the team

**What We Look For**  

Our ideal candidate embodies our values and brings:


* A genuine love for culinary experiences \& a passion for Japanese cuisine
* Proven experience as an assistant restaurant manager in a luxury high\-volume restaurant
* An approachable, motivational leader who thrives in nurturing team talent
* Naturally friendly, customer\-oriented, and skilled at creating memorable dining experiences

**Benefits**  

We offer incredible career growth, international opportunities, and the chance to contribute to a world\-renowned brand, where every detail from the finest ingredients to our unrivalled atmosphere is crafted to perfection.


As part of our team, you’ll enjoy:

* **World\-Class training,** designed to inspire and educate
* **Global opportunities**, experience hospitality around the globe with our five incredible brands
* **Family Meals** are shared daily
* **Rewards \& Recognition,** we value our team and celebrate your contributions to our success with meaningful rewards and recognition
* **Dining discount,** enjoy exclusive discounts across the Azumi group, worldwide!
  




  

**Our Commitment to Inclusivity**  

We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome all applicants. If you require any assistance to make the recruitment process more accessible, please let one of our Talent Acquisition team know.


INDMANAGE

title: Human Resources Manager
description:
FlexAutο is growing and we are looking for and experienced and passionate **HR Manager** to join our team in **Rome, Italy**. This is a unique opportunity to play a key role in shaping the HR function of the fastest\-growing Greek scale\-up, and one of Europe’s most exciting companies in mobility innovation.

**Who We Are**


FlexAuto is more than a car leasing company. We redefine the way consumers own a car by offering flexible and accessible terms and an all inclusive premium service. Our aim is to elevate the car leasing field and pave the way for the future of mobility in Greece, Italy, Cyprus, Mexico and across the globe.

**People at FlexAuto**


We are constantly looking for curious and ambitious minds that are eager to be part of an exciting journey and a very strong professional team, so if you are ready to kick off your career at one of the most promising European start\-ups, join us!

**Position Summary**


As part of our international HR team, the **HR Manager – Italy** will lead all core HR activities in our Italian operations, ensuring strong alignment with our global HR strategy while adapting practices to fit local regulations and cultural nuances. This is a **hands\-on role** that blends strategic thinking with day\-to\-day operational responsibilities.

**Responsibilities**

* Lead recruitment, selection, and onboarding processes for various departments.
* Act as a point of contact for employee queries, ensuring a supportive work environment.
* Implement and monitor performance review cycles and feedback mechanisms.
* Design and execute training programs to support career development and upskilling.
* Promote company culture through internal and external initiatives.
* Coordinate and support local corporate social responsibility and inclusion programs.
* Ensure adherence to Italian labor law and internal HR policies.
* Maintain HR dashboards and reports to support data\-driven decision making.
* Support the rollout of global HR practices across our European teams.
  

  

  

**Requirements**  

* A Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field is required. A Master’s degree or additional HR certifications (e.g., CIPD, SHRM) will be considered a plus.
* Minimum of 5 years of progressive experience in HR, with at least 2 years in a managerial or senior generalist capacity, ideally within a dynamic, international, or fast\-scaling environment.
* Deep and up\-to\-date knowledge of Italian labor legislation, social security, and employment regulations, with the ability to ensure full compliance and advise confidently on legal matters.
* Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build trust, influence stakeholders at all levels, and handle sensitive matters with empathy and discretion.
* Full professional fluency in both Italian and English, spoken and written, is essential.
* Comfortable operating in a fast\-paced, entrepreneurial setting where priorities can shift quickly. You are proactive, solution\-oriented, and thrive under ambiguity.
* Proficiency in HR software platforms (e.g., HRIS, ATS) and strong command of Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, PowerPoint, Word). Experience with HR analytics and reporting tools is a plus.
  

  

  

**Benefits**  

* Opportunity to work in one of the fastest growing scaleups
* A working environment with an amazing culture that is based on Respect, Fairness, and Transparency
* Private Health Insurance
* Free coffee and beverages
* Car benefits
* Ongoing training and development
* Competitive salary

*All applications will be treated in utmost confidence and will only be seen by members of our recruitment team.*

title: International Total Rewards Regional Support
description:
Rome, Latium


Job ID JR2026503728 Category Human Resources Role Type Hybrid Post Date Apr. 01, 2026  

**Job Description**


At Boeing, we innovate and collaborate to make the world a better place. We’re committed to fostering an environment for every teammate that’s welcoming, respectful and inclusive, with great opportunity for professional growth. Find your future with us.


We are looking for an experienced and detail\-oriented **International Total Rewards Regional Support** to join the team in **Rome, Italy.**


This role will support the Boeing International Total Rewards team by acting in a support capacity for our TR leaders in Europe, UKI and METACA as well as chief of staff duties for the broader team.


Total Rewards regional support will include market and trend analytics, development of Total Rewards policies and processes, survey submissions, market range development, and general implementation and maintenance of compensation and benefits programs, policies, procedures, processes ensuring locally competitive programs that meet business objectives. Chief of staff duties to include LEAN focal responsibilities, management and maintenance of team resources, and project planning duties. Role will participate in Global and Enterprise projects as well.


This position is **hybrid**. This means that the selected candidate will be required to perform some work onsite at one of the listed location options. This is at the hiring team’s discretion and could potentially change in the future.

**Position responsibilities:**  

The successful candidate will perform the following duties:

* Support the ongoing development and delivery of compensation and benefit programs
* Conduct ad hoc analyses, research and data collation to support compensation and benefit projects
* Establish and execute annual benefit communication plans
* Support education and communication on compensation and benefit matters
* Maintain, Draft and coordinate new and updated Policies/ Pay Practices documentation. Provide interpretation on Policies/ Pay practices as required.
* Keep abreast of changes to relevant laws and initiate action necessary to keep Policies/Pay Practice up to date.
* Establish implementation schedules and coordinate with internal teams (e.g. Payroll, Communications, HR, Legal, Timekeeping) to implement domestic benefit changes
* Compile data and respond to benefit surveys
* Coordinate compensation approvals, deviations or changes
* Participate in local/international project teams with cross\-functional representation
* Manage and maintain team resources with a LEAN mindset including OneNote, SharePoints, and servers.
* Project management duties for Global Initiatives


This position requires some international travel and regular participation in international calls/meetings which may occur outside of standard business hours.

**Basic Qualifications (Required Skills/Experience)**:

* Relevant HR and Total Rewards experience
* Experience working in a dynamic Global Company across various HR, compensation or benefits programs and strategies
* Experience with HR, compensation and benefits strategy, programs and projects
* General knowledge of applicable Compensation and Benefits Practices, Market Trends and application of required local labor laws as it relates to Compensation
* Skill and ability to collect, organize, synthesize, and analyze data; summarize findings; develop conclusions and recommendations from appropriate data sources with clients, customers and/or suppliers.
* Proven ability to engage and collaborate with internal/external stakeholders globally through calls and discussions would be a pre\-requisite for the role together with excellent project and time management skills.
* Effective influencing skills working with managers and supervisors.
* Demonstrated experience in effective verbal and written and written communication e.g. Listens to and works to understand others needs or requirements. Communicates confidently and persuasively with a clear sense of audience and appropriate tone. Demonstrates logical thought processes in communications. Articulates complex issues in a concise and readily understood manner. Articulates thoughts in a clear, effective and organized manner.
* This position will be required to travel (approximately 10%), and work in a global team with regular participation in international calls/ meetings, which will include outside of standard business hours.

**Preferred Experience (Skills and Qualifications)**

* Working in a Regional/ International Team, with experience contributing and rolling out of globally developed strategy and projects
* Experience with Workday HR Systems
* M\&A/ integration experience
* Bachelor degree or higher

**Work Authorization:**


This requisition is for a locally hired position in Italy. Candidates must have current legal authorisation to work immediately in Italy. Boeing will not attempt to obtain Immigration and labour sponsorship for any applicants. Benefits and pay are determined at the local level and are not part of Boeing U.S. based payroll.

**Conflict of Interest**


Successful candidates for this job must satisfy the Company's Conflict of Interest (COI) assessment process.

  


Applications for this position will be accepted until **Apr. 04, 2026**

  

**Export Control Requirements:**

This is not an Export Control position.

**Relocation**

Relocation assistance is not a negotiable benefit for this position.

**Visa Sponsorship**

Employer will not sponsor applicants for employment visa status.

**Shift**

Not a Shift Worker (Italy)

  

**Equal Opportunity Employer:**


We are an equal opportunity employer. We do not accept unlawful discrimination in our recruitment or employment practices on any grounds including but not limited to; race, color, ethnicity, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, physical or mental disability, genetic factors, military and veteran status, or other characteristics covered by applicable law.


We have teams in more than 65 countries, and each person plays a role in helping us become one of the world’s most innovative, diverse and inclusive companies. We are proud members of the Valuable 500 and welcome applications from candidates with disabilities. Applicants are encouraged to share with our recruitment team any accommodations required during the recruitment process. Accommodations may include but are not limited to: conducting interviews in accessible locations that accommodate mobility needs, encouraging candidates to bring and use any existing assistive technology such as screen readers and offering flexible interview formats such as virtual or phone interviews.


  
**Your Benefits**
-----------------


No matter where you are in life, our benefits help prepare you for the present and the future.


* Competitive base pay and incentive programs.
* Industry\-leading tuition assistance program pays your institution directly.
* Resources and opportunities to grow your career.
* Up to $10,000 match when you support your favorite nonprofit organizations.

description:
Job Posting: 27/Mar/2026
Closure Date: 10/Apr/2026, 4:59:00 PM
Organizational Unit: CSH
Job Type: Non\-staff opportunities
Type of Requisition: Consultant
Grade Level: N/A
Primary Location: Italy\-Rome
Duration: Initially for 6 months with possibility of extension – maximum duration 11 months
Post Number: N/A
IMPORTANT NOTICE: Please note that Closure Date and Time displayed above are based on date and time settings of your personal device  

* FAO is committed to achieving workforce diversity in terms of gender, nationality, background and culture.
* Qualified female applicants, qualified nationals of non\-and under\-represented Members and person with disabilities are encouraged to apply;
* Everyone who works for FAO is required to adhere to the highest standards of integrity and professional conduct, and to uphold FAO's values
* FAO, as a Specialized Agency of the United Nations, has a zero\-tolerance policy for conduct that is incompatible with its status, objectives and mandate, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination
* All selected candidates will undergo rigorous reference and background checks
* All applications will be treated with the strictest confidentiality

*FAO’s commitment to environmental sustainability is integral to our strategic objectives and operations.* **Organizational Setting**


FAO’s Human Resources Division (CSH) provides HR strategies, policies, tools, guidance and oversight to FAO clients worldwide to ensure that the Organization attracts, develops and retains a diverse, skilled and highly motivated workforce, delivering on the mandate of the organization.


The Strategy and Policy Team Branch (CSHP) develops and drafts strategy\-related documents and policies in human resources management; coordinates consultations with internal stakeholders on actions to develop and implement HR policies; determines proper interpretation of policies, and human resources related Staff Regulations, Staff Rules and Administrative Manual sections falling under its mandate; provides guidance and support to the HR servicing functions in the Shared Service Centre (SSC) and the Regional Offices.


The assignment is located within **CSHP.**

 **Reporting Lines**


The Specialist reports to the Human Resources Officer (Administrative Law and Workplace Relations).

 **Technical Focus**


HR policies relevant to international organizations, employment law and international administrative law.


Comprehensive HR advice to managers and staff, and support to the Human Resources Officer (Administrative Law and Workplace Relations), the Human Resources Officer (Policy), the Senior Human Resources Officer (Chief CSHP), and the Director, Human Resources Division as necessary on disciplinary cases, performance matters, and policy development.

 **Tasks and responsibilities**

* Review requests for, and provide, HR advice to managers and other personnel on workplace relations issues including performance management, recommendations for non\-renewal or termination of contracts, and interpretation of policies regarding benefits and entitlements.
* Liaise with the Office of the Inspector\-General (OIG), Ethics Office, Ombudsperson, the Legal Office and the SSC on disciplinary or other complex cases of unsatisfactory conduct.
* Review and prepare memoranda and other communications in cases of unsatisfactory conduct.
* Research relevant FAO rules, regulations and policies on HR matters.
* Assist in the preparation of policy and position papers, as well as briefing notes, on complex administrative HR matters.
* Assist in the review and development of HR strategies and policies, particularly in the areas of sexual harassment, harassment and abuse of authority.

 **CANDIDATES WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINST THE FOLLOWING**

 **Minimum Requirements**

* Advanced university degree in Law, Administration or Human Resources from an institution recognized by the International Association of Universities (IAU/UNESCO); OR Bachelor’s degree from an institution recognized by the International Association of Universities (IAU/UNESCO) and a post\-graduate professional qualification in Law; candidates with only a Bachelor's degree who do not have an advanced university degree or professional qualification in Law would need two additional years of relevant professional experience.
* At least five years of work experience in the field of Law, of which at least two years should be in reviewing and advising on administrative and/or employment law matters, and at least one year with an international organization.
* Working knowledge (Level C \- proficiency) of English.

 **FAO Core Competencies**

* Results Focus
* Teamwork
* Communication
* Building Effective Relationships
* Knowledge Sharing and Continuous Improvement

 **Technical/Functional Skills**

* Experience in administrative and employment law, including working on disciplinary, performance management and complex individual administrative law cases at the international level.
* Experience in development and implementation of human resources strategies, policies and procedures.
* Knowledge of another FAO language would be an asset.

  

Please note that all candidates should adhere to FAO Values of Commitment to FAO, Respect for All and Integrity and Transparency

 **ADDITIONAL INFORMATION*** FAO does not charge any fee at any stage of the recruitment process (application, interview, processing)
* Please note that FAO will only consider academic credentials or degrees obtained from an educational institution recognized in the IAU/UNESCO list
* Please note that FAO only considers higher educational qualifications obtained from an institution accredited/recognized in the World Higher Education Database (WHED), a list updated by the International Association of Universities (IAU) / United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO). The list can be accessed at http://www.whed.net/
* For more information, visit the FAO employment website
* Appointment will be subject to certification that the candidate is medically fit for appointment, accreditation, any residency or visa requirements, and security clearances.

 **HOW TO APPLY*** To apply, visit the recruitment website at Jobs at FAO and complete your online profile. We strongly recommend that your profile is accurate, complete and includes your employment records, academic qualifications, and language skills
* Candidates are requested to attach a letter of motivation to the online profile
* Once your profile is completed, please apply, and submit your application
* Please note that FAO only considers higher educational qualifications obtained from an institution accredited/recognized in the World Higher Education Database (WHED), a list updated by the International Association of Universities (IAU) / United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO). The list can be accessed at http://www.whed.net/. These qualifications should be in alignment with the International Standard Classification of Education (ISCED) mappings.

* Candidates may be requested to provide performance assessments and authorization to conduct verification checks of past and present work, character, education, military and police records to ascertain any and all information which may be pertinent to the employment qualifications
* Incomplete applications will not be considered
* Personal information provided on your application may be shared within FAO and with other companies acting on FAO’s behalf to provide employment support services such as pre\-screening of applications, assessment tests, background checks and other related services. You will be asked to provide your consent before submitting your application. You may withdraw consent at any time, by withdrawing your application, in such case FAO will no longer be able to consider your application
* Only applications received through the FAO recruitment portal will be considered
* Your application will be screened based on the information provided in your online profile
* We encourage applicants to submit the application well before the deadline date.

If you need help or have queries, please create a one\-time registration with FAO’s client support team for further assistance: **https://fao.service\-now.com/csp**

description:

**HRMPS** è una realtà dinamica e innovativa che nasce dall’esperienza di oltre 20 anni di attività professionale nella consulenza specializzata in gestione delle risorse umane, fornendo supporto mirato in ambito paghe e compliance, accompagnando le aziende con soluzioni affidabili e su misura.

Forniamo soluzioni personalizzate per semplificare i processi aziendali e ottimizzare la gestione del personale.

Il nostro team, preciso e affidabile, supporta aziende di ogni dimensione con un servizio professionale e orientato ai risultati. La nostra missione è migliorare l’efficienza operativa e garantire una gestione HR conforme alle normative, mettendo al centro il personale. Con HRMPS, le aziende possono contare su competenza e attenzione per un ambiente di lavoro sereno e produttivo.

**Descrizione del ruolo**

Cerchiamo un praticante consulente del lavoro per entrare a far parte del team HRMPS \- Human Resource Management \& Payroll Specialist, con sede di lavoro a Roma. In questo ruolo a tempo pieno e in sede, supporterai le attività quotidiane relative alla gestione delle risorse umane e dei contratti di lavoro. Le responsabilità includeranno l'assistenza nella redazione e revisione dei contratti di lavoro, l'approfondimento delle normative giuslavoristiche, e la consulenza su tematiche riguardanti i rapporti di lavoro. Inoltre, contribuirai attivamente a risolvere problematiche in ambito lavorativo, mantenendo un approccio professionale e orientato alla collaborazione. Ma non solo, anche supporto nell’elaborazione e gestione dei cedolini paga e relativi adempimenti fiscali e contributivi, consulenza operativa ai clienti in materia giuslavoristica, sulla contrattualistica aziendale, sugli adempimenti, sugli ammortizzatori (indennità di mobilità, cassa integrazione, indennità di disoccupazione; contratti di solidarietà, ecc.), sulle relazioni sindacali, gestione degli adempimenti annuali concernenti la gestione del personale dipendente (modelli CU – Modello 770 – Autoliquidazione INAIL – F24 etc.) e degli adempimenti periodici (Modello Uniemens, etc.), gestione di pratiche di assunzione, trasformazione e cessazione dei rapporti di lavoro, elaborazione comunicazioni online con centri per l’impiego, istituti ed enti di previdenza e altri adempimenti legati al ruolo di Praticante al fine del buon esito del percorso formativo e abilitante.

Il tutto coadiuvato dal tuo Domunis.

**Qualifiche**

* Conoscenze in gestione delle risorse umane e people management per supportare le attività organizzative e relazionali.
* Competenze in contratti di lavoro e diritto del lavoro e dell'occupazione, con capacità di applicare e interpretare le normative giuslavoristiche.
* Esperienza o interesse nell'ambito delle relazioni di lavoro e della consulenza, con focus sulla comunicazione chiara e risoluzione di problematiche legate ai rapporti di lavoro.
* Attitudine all'apprendimento, precisione e capacità di lavorare in team con approccio orientato al cliente.
* Esperienza con software paghe Gruppo Centro Paghe e HR management

**Chi cerchiamo**

1\. Laurea in Consulenza del lavoro, in Giurisprudenza o in Economia, con ottimo *curriculum studiorum*; con disponibilità a svolgere il praticantato con decorrenza immediata (vedasi nello specifico quanto riportato nel seguente link: https://www.cnoconsulentidellavoro.it/component/k2/accesso\-alla\-professione);

2\. Passione per il Diritto del Lavoro maturata durante il percorso di studio o affinata in successive esperienze formative;

3\. Conoscenza dei principati Istituti del Diritto del Lavoro e aggiornamento sulle novità normative in materia;

4\. Ottime capacità relazionali e comunicative; cortesia;

5\. Capacità di gestire lo stress;

6\. Capacità di organizzare le proprie attività, di gestire una agenda, di svolgere più attività in contemporanea;

7\. Forte propensione all’approfondimento; capacità analitiche, rigore e precisione;

8\. Ottimo uso PC, pacchetto Office: Word, Excel, posta elettronica;

9\. Domicilio a Roma o provincia, e comunque in aree limitrofe alla sede dello studio;

10\. Automunita/o o Motomunita/o.

**Compenso**

E' previsto un rimborso spese pari a 800,00 € incrementabile durante il praticantato, a fronte della crescita professionale al raggiungimento di obiettivi individuali e dell'organizzazione.

Per tutte le attività sopra indicate sarà fondamentale la contemporanea attività di formazione.

**Contatti**

I c.v. dovranno pervenire sul seguente indirizzo mail: **risorse@managementhr.it**

Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91\.

Contratto di lavoro: Tirocinio formativo/stage  
Durata contratto: 6 mesi

Retribuzione: a partire da €800,00 al mese

Benefit:

* Computer aziendale
* Convenzioni aziendali
* Supporto allo sviluppo professionale

Sede di lavoro: Di persona

Data di inizio prevista: 01/05/2026

title: HR Business Partner, EU One PXT
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


\*\*This role is based fulltime in Jesi, relocation package can be offered if required\*\*  

  

\*\*Questo ruolo è basato a tempo pieno a Jesi, può essere offerto un pacchetto di trasferimento se necessario\*\*  

  

Creating a culture where all our people feel encouraged and supported is what makes Amazon a great place to work. As an HR Business Partner, you’ll drive a positive employee experience through your detailed grasp of HR programmes, processes, and tools, offering credible and clear HR advice to Amazon’s managers. From resolving complex HR queries with colleagues to perfecting new policies and programmes, you’ll be making a real difference in how Amazon operates.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

Own and deliver projects and drive progress towards business goals  

Engage and coordinate with key stakeholders on data collection and policy implementation  

Resolve high\-level employee issues, including grievances, appeals and complex disciplinary issues  

Coach and develop HR colleagues and stakeholders across the business  

Where relevant, co\-ordinate with work councils and unions to drive positive employee relations  

Analyse internal dashboards to create reports on key metrics for leadership at your site  

  

A day in the life  

HR Business Partners get involved in a wide range of different projects and work with a mix of diverse stakeholders, all with one goal: to make Amazon a great place to work. You’ll be based in an Amazon fulfilment centre, delivery station or sortation centre and will frequently use data to inform key decisions.  

  

From one day to the next, you could be taking the lead on a range of different projects or supporting senior stakeholders with developing strategies and carrying out regular talent reviews. You’ll be taking a fresh look at our working culture and thinking of new ways to optimise them.  

  

You’ll also be supporting new colleagues to develop and learn new skills, so as well as shaping our working culture, you’ll be helping our colleagues grow. There will be plenty of opportunities for you to progress and shape your career at Amazon.  

  

About the team  

At Amazon, our HR department plays a vital role in looking after our people. Known as the People Experience and Technology Solutions (PXT) team, our priority is making the Amazon experience brilliant for our people, helping us remain one of the world’s most innovative, customer\-centric and nimble businesses.  

  

We operate proactively and take a holistic approach to people management. Our team is ‘Customer Obsessed’ and focuses on solving employee engagement issues quickly, ensuring nothing impacts our seamless employee and customer experience. Ultimately, it’s our job to insist on the highest standards and drive progress across Amazon’s strategy and goals.

**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* 1\. What are your yearly salary expectations?
* 2\. Are you currently located in, or willing to relocate to, the location of this position?
* 3\. Are you willing and able to be onsite Monday through Friday (5 days a week) for this role?
* 1\. What are your monthly gross salary expectations?

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* Do you have experience with MS Office suite (Word, Excel, Outlook) in a professional environment?

  

Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.  

  

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Addetto/a Risorse Umane – Società di Consulenza HR
Human Capital Consultant – Categoria Protetta L.68/99
description:
***Entra nel nostro mondo.***  

***Trasforma insieme a noi.***

***Sarai tu ad entrare nel team?***

Ti interessa lo sviluppo del potenziale umano e alle dinamiche cooperative all'interno delle organizzazioni? Hai un interesse particolare per la gestione delle risorse umane e per lo sviluppo e la formazione? Se sì, potresti essere la persona giusta per unirti al nostro team Human Capital!


Avrai l'opportunità di immergerti nelle complesse dinamiche delle risorse umane e contribuire al nostro impegno nel coltivare un ambiente lavorativo positivo e produttivo.


**Responsabilità:**

* Collaborare con il team Human Capital nella gestione di progetti HR per aziende clienti e per il personale interno di Bip;
* Partecipare a progetti di revisione processi e/o analisi di requisiti, riorganizzazione HR o aziendali;
* Contribuire alla gestione di progetti dedicati al Change Management e Adoption Sistemi;
* Contribuire alla gestione di progetti dedicati al Change Management;
* Supportare le attività quotidiane per lo sviluppo e la crescita del team Human Capital.

**Requisiti:**

* Iscrizione alle liste Categorie Protette Art.1 LG 68/99;
* Esperienza di almeno 3 anni, preferibilmente all’interno di società di consulenza;
* Laurea in Economia Gestionale, Matematica o Psicologia ed indirizzi affini;
* Conoscenza dei principali sistemi HCM Payroll e Time Management (SAP, Zucchetti, ADP, F2A, Inaz) o altri prodotti paghe e rilevazione presenze;
* Passione per lo sviluppo delle risorse umane e la formazione;
* Eccellenti capacità comunicative e relazionali;
* Capacità di lavorare in team e in un ambiente dinamico;
* Ottima padronanza del pacchetto Office;
* Buona propensione digitale (Es. AI);
* Conoscenza consolidata della lingua inglese sia scritta che parlata.

Costituirà un plus l’aver svolto Master in ambito HR.


Se sei pronto/a a fare la differenza e a contribuire alla crescita delle risorse umane in un contesto aziendale stimolante, questo annuncio fa per te! Unisciti a noi e sperimenta un'opportunità unica di sviluppo delle competenze nel campo delle risorse umane.


**Perchè Bip?**

***People at the center of our culture***

Fiducia e collaborazione, imprenditorialità e coraggio, meritocrazia e sviluppo guidano la crescita delle nostre persone.


Lavorare in BIP è un'esperienza sfidante, in cui il merito paga e l'innovazione avviene attraverso idee coraggiose, collaborazione e un rapporto di fiducia con i nostri clienti.


***Challenge yourself***

Avrai la possibilità di metterti alla prova in contesti progettuali diversi in termini di ambito, natura delle attività e stakeholders coinvolti. Vedrai valorizzato il tuo spirito di iniziativa, la tua passione, l’autonomia e la capacità di assumerti responsabilità e metterti in gioco.


***Unlocking your potential***

Potrai partecipare a \+ di 300 corsi formativi all’avanguardia per accrescere le tue competenze su tecnologie e temi di business emergenti.


***Work\-life Integration***

Troverai una policy che supporta il lavoro AGILE fino al 100% del proprio tempo e favorisce il work\-life integration.


***Diversity \& Inclusion***

Valorizziamo l’unicità e ci impegniamo a garantire che tutti i dipendenti abbiano pari opportunità di dare un contributo ed esprimere al massimo il potenziale nell'ambiente di lavoro


Il nostro approccio alla diversità e all’inclusione si fonda sui principi di etica e integrità e rappresenta il fattore determinante per lo sviluppo di orizzonti, di crescita personale e aziendale, più ampi.


Promuoviamo l’inserimento e l’integrazione lavorativa delle persone appartenenti alle categorie protette – in base a quanto disciplinato dalla legge 68/99


**Next Steps**

Una volta ricevuto il tuo cv ci prenderemo del tempo per valutarlo attentamente.


Se c’è un match con questa o con altre posizioni aperte all’interno del Gruppo, ti contatteremo per iniziare la nostra conoscenza reciproca.


L’iter di selezione comprende generalmente un colloquio con HR, un colloquio tecnico con un line manager ed un eventuale colloquio con un Partner.


L’ordine e il numero delle interviste potrebbero variare a seconda delle esigenze di recruiting.


In alternativa, saremo felici di conservare il tuo CV nel nostro database per poterlo rivalutare per altre opportunità future.


Se dovessi identificare ulteriori posizioni di tuo interesse ed in linea con il tuo profilo, aspettiamo la tua candidatura per poterla valutare nuovamente.


**Su di Noi**

Nati nel 2003, abbiamo raccolto e valorizzato l’esperienza storica della consulenza e abbiamo aggiunto due ingredienti chiave: l’innovazione e la digitalizzazione.


Grazie a questo percorso siamo oggi oltre 5500 professionisti e professioniste, presenti in 13 Paesi, con oltre 4500 progetti alle spalle e le conoscenze più all’avanguardia nell'ambito della Digital Transformation, Data Science, Cybersecurity, Industry 4\.0, IOT e di tutte le Disruptive Technologies che mettiamo a servizio di ogni settore di mercato.


Aiutiamo i nostri clienti a fare la differenza creando qualità su larga scala attraverso una formula che opera su tre leve: Valore, Persone e Tecnologia.


Crediamo nel valore dell’eccellenza, che è la bussola su cui orientiamo il nostro agire, e adottiamo un approccio etico e leale nei confronti di quante e quanti scelgono di lavorare con noi, promuovendo un ambiente in cui le persone possano crescere insieme, grazie alla contaminazione tra competenze diverse.

title: Senior Technical Consultant
description:
*To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.*

Job Category


Customer Success
Job Details


**About Salesforce**

Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.


Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.


**DEPARTMENT DESCRIPTION**  

  

Salesforce Professional Services focuses on delivering strategic engagements that define transformational opportunities and articulate clear plans for execution of change programs. We rely on our team’s expertise and specialisms to engage with executive\-level customers to agree on specific business goals and actions to ignite the full value of Salesforce. Our approach considers people, process, and technology — and a successful engagement includes recommendations to the business for change to support deeper, more relevant use of the Salesforce platform.
  

Salesforce Technical Consultants deliver solutions that transform our customers’ businesses and their customers’ experiences. This role involves working with teams to discover, design, code and deploy Salesforce Platform solutions.
  

  

**OVERVIEW OF THE ROLE**  

  

Salesforce is currently seeking a Senior Technical Consultant to join our Professional Services team within the Customer Success Group (CSG). Senior Technical Consultants work as part of a team demonstrating technical thought leadership of customer and partner engagements. These lead to the successful delivery of solutions, including enterprise integrations using middleware tools and platforms. Through our extended onboarding program of formal certification, internal training, knowledge transfer, hands on experience and closely working with other Consultants, Architects and Project Managers you will become a Salesforce Domain Authority at the forefront of our offerings, delivering outstanding customer success.
  

  

Senior Technical Consultants are technically hands on with our products, and are responsible for evaluating and implementing technical solutions for customers.
  

The ideal Senior Technical Consultant is a demonstrated thought leader in technical aspects of customer and partner engagements that lead to the successful delivery of enterprise desktop, cloud, and/or mobile solutions, including enterprise integrations leveraging middleware tools and platforms. They also identify and proactively handle risk areas and contribute to seeing issues through to project completion.
  

  

**YOUR IMPACT**  

  

Through our extended onboarding program of formal certification, internal training, knowledge transfer, hands on experience and closely working with other Consultants, Architects and Project Managers you will become a Salesforce Domain Authority at the forefront of our offerings, delivering outstanding customer success.


* Become a highly certified and recognized Salesforce expert.
* Help clients consistently meet/exceed goals and metrics.
* Contribute to customer success through front and backend solution development and release management.
* Deliver high client satisfaction (CSAT).
* Provide guidance on the application and integration development best practices, Enterprise Architecture standards, functional and technical solution architecture \& design, environment management, testing, and Salesforce Platform education.
* Drive the creation of application and technical design documents which leverage Salesforce best practices and effectively integrate Salesforce into the customer’s infrastructure.
* Troubleshoot key customer implementation issues and demonstrate the ability to drive to a successful resolution. Lead the evaluation of business and technical requirements from a senior level.
* Identification and proactive management of risk areas and commitment to seeing an issue through to complete resolution.
* Manage customer expectations; negotiate solutions to complex problems with both the customer and third\-party partners.
Build relationships with business partners, internal and external, and contributing to broader goals and growth beyond the scope of a single customer engagement.  
* 

  

  

**REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS**

* 5 years of experience minimum in IT space
* Hands\-on technical and solution implementation experience in a relevant software domain, as well as coding experience in object oriented languages.
* Ability to analyze, design, and optimize business processes via technology and integration.
* Ability to support customers and project members in rationalising and deploying emerging technology.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a distributed team.
* Ability to learn new technologies as well as good presentation, communication and facilitation skills.
* Available to travel regionally and occasionally internationally depending on customer needs.
* Degree or equivalent relevant experience required. Experience will be evaluated based on the core competencies for the role (e.g. extracurricular leadership roles, military experience, volunteer roles, work experience, etc.)
* Proficiency in both English AND Italian languages
Strong background in object\-oriented programming (e.g., Java) and understanding of REST/SOAP API design, microservices, and event\-driven architectures.  
* 

  

  

**PREFERRED**

* Salesforce Certifications
* Experience with the Salesforce Platform and / or other CRM products.
* Experience with Salesforce Revenue Cloud and / or other products in the billing and revenue space (Conga, CloudSense, Zuora).
* Ability to build and manage relationships both internally and externally.
* Experience working in a senior consulting or technical lead role working with matrix teams at a top\-tier consultancy or system integrator.
* Familiarity with AI\-assisted development tools

Unleash Your Potential


When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.


Accommodations


If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form .


Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options.


Posting Statement


Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Qualification / Validation Specialist
description:
for the site Borgo San Michele LT, Italy


Would you like to make a valuable contribution to the health of patients? And do something really meaningful on your own responsibility? Then we look forward to hearing from you! Excellence beyond manufacturing \- that's what we stand for as Aenova, one of the world's leading contract manufacturers and developers for the pharmaceutical industry with 4,000 employees at 15 sites. Latina is a leading centre of excellence in sterile liquid production, with a strategic investment plan to become one of Europe's foremost hubs.**Your key responsibilities**
-----------------------------


We’re currently looking to expand our Quality and Engineering teams with experts in Qualification, as Qualification Specialist, Requalification Specialist and QA Equipment Validation Specialist.
  

As we are recruiting for several roles, feel free to apply even if your profile does not match with all the requirements below.


**PURPOSE AND RESPONSIBILITIES**  

The purpose of these roles is to ensure the qualification status of the production equipment, QC instrument \& equipment and Warehouse through the definition of procedures/protocols and the execution of requalification activities, in compliance with applicable regulations (laws, GMP, FDA, etc.), the guidelines contained in the dossier, and company quality standards.


* Review master and complete qualification and validation protocols, summary reports and associated data for conformance to regulations, SOPs, specifications and other applicable acceptance criteria.
* Troubleshoot / investigate validation\-related deviations and non\-conformances.
* Maintain organization and archival of completed validation and qualification document packages.
* Prepare, review, and maintain GMP documentation, including URS, DQ, IQ, OQ, PQ, qualification protocols, and final reports, in accordance with internal procedures and regulatory expectations.
* Interface and coordinate qualification activities with Engineering, Production, Quality Assurance, Maintenance, MSAT, and HSE functions within a CDMO environment.
* Provide support during internal audits, client audits, and regulatory inspections (EMA, FDA), including periodic review of qualified systems.
* Ensure the analysis and evaluation of raw technical data and documentation related to equipment and instruments subject to requalification, in order to acquire the information necessary for defining procedures/protocols and carrying out re\-qualification activities, in compliance with company procedures.
* In collaboration with the Purchasing Department, prepare requests for quotation to suppliers and support the Manager in preparing a technical and economic evaluation of the offers received.

**Your profile**
----------------

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a scientific or engineering discipline (e.g. Chemistry, Biotechnology, Engineering, Pharmacy).
* 2–3 years of experience in qualification and/or validation roles within pharmaceutical or biotechnology manufacturing environments, preferably in CDMO organizations.
* Solid knowledge of GMP and relevant international regulatory standards (EMA, FDA).
* Proven experience in the preparation and management of GMP documentation in regulated environments.
* Hands\-on experience gained in pharmaceutical production areas utilizing isolator technology, particularly in aseptic and sterile Fill \& Finish operations for biologics preferred.
* Knowledge of regulations related to Equipment and Utilities validation, with particular reference to sterile departments (for examples isolator, autoclave, tunnel, VHP).
* Technical/engineering knowledge of sterile lyophilized pharmaceutical production systems.
* Strong attention to detail, accuracy, and ability to manage qualification activities independently.
* Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively in cross\-functional and client\-facing environments.
* Good command of the English language (minimum B2 level).
* Proficiency in standard IT tools, including MS Office, document management systems, and ERP platforms.

**Your motivation**
-------------------


Are you looking for new challenges in a highly competitive environment? And you want to tackle them creatively and on your own responsibility? Do you prefer a "get\-it\-done" culture and think in terms of solutions rather than problems? What are you waiting for? We would be happy to explain our corporate benefits in a personal conversation!
If you have any questions, I \- Luca Cenciarini / Human Resources \- will be happy to help you: luca.cenciarini@aenova\-group.com

title: Commerciali
description:
Siamo un'azienda italiana operante nella ricerca e selezione del personale che si occupa di soddisfare le necessità dei clienti offrendo servizi personalizzati.

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

Ricerchiamo **commerciali** per la promozione e la vendita di un **gestionale dedicato alle agenzie immobiliari**.

 **Attività principali**


La figura selezionata si occuperà di:

* Promuovere e vendere il gestionale presso le agenzie immobiliari del territorio assegnato
* Presentare il prodotto, illustrandone caratteristiche, funzionalità e vantaggi
* Supportare il cliente nella fase di avviamento, garantendo una corretta conoscenza del software
* Fornire assistenza commerciale e operativa di primo livello, quando necessario

**Requisiti:**
--------------

**Requisiti richiesti**

* Esperienza in ambito commerciale, preferibilmente nel settore immobiliare o nei servizi/software
* Spiccate capacità comunicative e relazionali
